title: Lab 2 – Navigation - 2026
instructions: Perform all tasks from your home directory unless specified otherwise. Use proper Linux filesystem navigation, file management, copy, redirection, and text viewing commands.
questions: Create the following nested directory structure in your home directory: 'vars/systems/logs'.
Inside the 'vars/systems' directory, create an empty file named 'dmesg'.
Create a file named 'file1.txt' inside the 'vars/systems/logs' directory containing the text: 'I love Linux and am excited to join the DevOps course'.
Create another nested directory structure in your home directory: 'vars/os/configs'.
Copy the '/etc/hosts' file into the 'vars/os/configs' directory.
Copy the hosts file from 'vars/os/configs' into the 'vars' directory located in your home directory.
Use the cat command to display the contents of the 'hosts' file located in the 'vars' directory.
Use the cat command again on the same hosts file and redirect the output into a new file named 'new_hosts' stored under the 'vars/os/configs' directory.
Copy the 'file1.txt' file from the 'vars/systems/logs' directory into the 'vars/os' directory and rename it to 'new_file1.txt' during the copy operation.
Inside the logs directory, create two empty files named 'sshd_config' and 'httpd.conf'.
Create a new file named 'new_file.txt' in the 'vars/systems' directory containing the text: 'I am enjoying DeveOps sessions and lab work'.
Attempt to remove the 'vars/os/configs' directory using the rmdir command and observe whether it succeeds or fails.
Use the tail command to display the last 5 lines of '/etc/nsswitch.conf' and append the output into a file named 'switch_output.txt' located in the 'vars/systems' directory.
Use the head command to display the first 3 lines of '/etc/resolv.conf' and overwrite the contents of a file named 'dns_output.txt' located in the 'vars/systems' directory.
Use the file command on '/etc/passwd' and '/bin/ls' and store the output into a file named 'output_file' inside the 'vars/systems/logs' directory.
